query_normalized: evaluating the robustness of llm-generated code to input perturbations computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) by measuring the same core mechanism of "robustness to input perturbations" (specifically adversarial examples) as the user's question, even though it focuses on model ensembles rather than LLM-generated code specifically. It also fits criterion (b) by providing empirical evidence on the dependent variable (robustness) under the domain of input perturbations, which is a foundational component of the user's research topic.
